source_jobs.title: Onboarding Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BLOCKIT

Blockit is a Sequoia-backed AI time agent. It learns your preferences and can autonomously coordinate meetings to any degree of complexity — timezones, group scheduling, IRL, and more — similar to an executive assistant, but one that never goes to sleep and responds instantly. It is the foundation of a massive platform of AI agents that will have access to the world’s time. The more people who link their calendars to Blockit, the more powerful our network becomes.




THE ROLE

Blockit is looking for an Onboarding Specialist to own the early prospect and customer experience. This entry-level role plays a critical part in the prospect and customer journey, and can grow into many different areas within Sales, Post Sales, and beyond.

You’ll be the first person new customers interact with after signing up — getting them set up, unlocking the product quickly, and making sure they’re seeing real value. You’ll also support pilots and GTM efforts, helping close customers by showing them what Blockit can do firsthand. You’ll work directly with founders, feed insights back to engineering and product, and help shape the onboarding playbook as we scale.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Run onboarding calls with new customers and guide them through setup and core workflows

 - Support pilots and early GTM efforts, helping prospects experience the product value firsthand

 - Build a deep understanding of the Blockit product so you can troubleshoot, advise, and educate confidently

 - Create onboarding materials, guides, and templates that scale the customer experience

 - Identify and flag product gaps, bugs, and customer insights back to the engineering and product teams

 - Be a genuine advocate for customers internally — their wins are your wins
   
   


WHO YOU ARE

 - A coachable team player who is hungry to learn and grow

 - A strong communicator — clear, warm, and able to explain technical concepts without making people feel dumb

 - Excited about AI and how it’s changing the way people work

 - Highly organized and detail-oriented — you follow through and nothing falls through the cracks

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and the speed of early-stage startup life

 - 6 months+ of tech sales or customer-facing experience, or 1+ years of any professional working experience preferred (internships count!)
   
   


BONUS POINTS

 - You use AI tools in your own workflow and are curious about where the space is going

 - Familiarity with tools like Notion, Slack, or Google Workspace

 - Interest in scheduling, productivity, or EA and ops workflows
   
   


WHY BLOCKIT

 - Real ownership and real impact from day one

 - Work directly with founders at a Sequoia-backed company

 - Clear path to grow into Sales, CS, or beyond

 - The work is meaningful — you’re helping people get time back

 - Competitive compensation for an early-stage role

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, AI Agents
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BLOCKIT

Time is the the most valuable resource we have, yet coordinating it remains stuck in the dark ages. At Blockit, we're building the AI that finally fixes this: an autonomous time agent that handles the full complexity of scheduling—timezones, group coordination, in-person logistics—like an executive assistant that never sleeps.

While every LLM application to date has been a one-on-one conversation, Blockit is one of the first multiplayer, stateful AI agents—coordinating between multiple people, maintaining context across conversations, and taking real actions in the world. As more people connect their calendars, our network becomes exponentially more powerful.

This is the foundation of a platform of AI agents with access to the world's time. We're backed by Sequoia, and we're a small, sharp team that moves fast, ships constantly, and holds a high bar. If you want to build something genuinely new, we'd love to talk.

You can visit our teams page https://www.notion.so/Meet-Blockit-Engineering-292f9049a24f803cb56dc84d4ece8730?pvs=21 to learn more about our team and culture!




THE ROLE

You’ll own the intelligence at the core of Blockit: our scheduling agents that autonomously coordinate meetings across people, time zones, and constraints.

This includes designing and iterating on agent architectures, writing and refining prompts, building evaluation frameworks, and shipping new capabilities as models improve. You’ll work across our orchestrator agents (which manage conversation flow) and specialized sub-agents, with the goal of making Blockit smarter, faster, and capable of handling increasingly complex coordination problems.

You’ll be architecting and building real-world AI agents used in production.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Write and refine prompts across our agent system—orchestrators, sub-agents, and tools

 - Build and maintain evals to measure agent quality and catch regressions

 - Debug agent failures: figure out why it misunderstood a request or made a bad call

 - Implement new agent capabilities as user needs expand

 - Experiment with new architectures and techniques as models improve

 - Instrument and analyze agent behavior to find patterns and failure modes


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2+ years of experience shipping and owning production software

 - Strong backend engineering skills, with the ability to work across the stack when needed

 - Experience working with LLMs in production systems (or a demonstrated ability to learn quickly in this space)

 - Deep curiosity about agent architectures and how the industry is evolving beyond simple prompt-based systems

 - Clear, structured communicator who can explain what’s working, what isn’t, and why


LOCATION

 - San Francisco, CA. On‑site 4 days per week

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BLOCKIT

Time is the most valuable resource we have, yet coordinating it remains stuck in the dark ages. At Blockit, we're building the AI that finally fixes this: an autonomous time agent that handles the full complexity of scheduling—timezones, group coordination, in-person logistics—like an executive assistant that never sleeps.

While every LLM application to date has been a one-on-one conversation, Blockit is one of the first multiplayer, stateful AI agents—coordinating between multiple people, maintaining context across conversations, and taking real actions in the world. As more people connect their calendars, our network becomes exponentially more powerful.

This is the foundation of a platform of AI agents with access to the world's time. We're backed by Sequoia, and we're a small, sharp team that moves fast, ships constantly, and holds a high bar. If you want to build something genuinely new, we'd love to talk.

You can visit our teams page https://www.notion.so/Meet-Blockit-Engineering-292f9049a24f803cb56dc84d4ece8730?pvs=21 to learn more about our team and culture.




THE ROLE

Think of this as a product engineer role — but the product is everything underneath. For a stateful, multiplayer AI agent that takes real actions in the world, infrastructure is the user experience. When a meeting gets scheduled in 800ms instead of 4 seconds, when an email never gets dropped, when our agent recovers gracefully from a flaky third-party API — that's a feature users feel. You'll ship those features.

This role spans three connected areas:

 - Infra that users feel — own our core systems (PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, async job processing, LLM gateway, email infra) with the same product instincts an engineer would bring to a customer-facing feature. Latency, reliability, and recovery are user-facing metrics, and you'll move them. We've outgrown some of our early choices and will outgrow more, so you'll also lead the build-vs-buy and migration calls: is pg-boss still the right queue or do we need a managed system? Should we move off Postmark? When do we add Redis, Kafka, a managed vector store? You make the call, ship the migration, and bring the team along.

 - Observability — own how we see our system. Metrics, logs, traces, dashboards, alerting, and the on-call / pager rotation — including what we alert on and what we don't. The goal is that we catch problems before customers do, and that when something does break, the engineer paged knows exactly where to look.

 - Developer experience & DevOps — own how Blockit engineers work day-to-day. That includes capacity planning and deploys, but also our AI-augmented dev environment: Claude Code setup, agent tooling, sandboxes, evals, the works. We want this team to be the most AI-native engineering org in the Valley and we need someone who treats that as a first-class problem.

This role is ideal if you care deeply about how things actually run in production and want to build the foundation for a platform that coordinates millions of calendars.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own, operate, and evolve our core data infrastructure: PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, and async processing pipelines — measuring success in user-facing terms (p95 scheduling latency, agent action success rate, message delivery reliability)

 - Own observability end-to-end: metrics, logs, traces, alerting, dashboards, and the on-call / pager rotation — including what we alert on and what we don't

 - Design, manage, and optimize our LLM infrastructure (gateway, evaluation pipelines, agent observability) for reliability, performance, and cost

 - Lead infrastructure migration decisions and execute them: e.g. evaluating pg-boss vs. managed queue alternatives, weighing whether to move off Postmark, introducing Redis / Kafka / similar when the time is right

 - Partner directly with product engineers to ship features — the line between "infra work" and "product work" should be invisible here

 - Own DevX: shape how engineers levera

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Product & Growth
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BLOCKIT

Time is the the most valuable resource we have, yet coordinating it remains stuck in the dark ages. At Blockit, we're building the AI that finally fixes this: an autonomous time agent that handles the full complexity of scheduling—timezones, group coordination, in-person logistics—like an executive assistant that never sleeps.

While every LLM application to date has been a one-on-one conversation, Blockit is one of the first multiplayer, stateful AI agents—coordinating between multiple people, maintaining context across conversations, and taking real actions in the world. As more people connect their calendars, our network becomes exponentially more powerful.

This is the foundation of a platform of AI agents with access to the world's time. We're backed by Sequoia, and we're a small, sharp team that moves fast, ships constantly, and holds a high bar. If you want to build something genuinely new, we'd love to talk.

You can visit our teams page https://www.notion.so/Meet-Blockit-Engineering-292f9049a24f803cb56dc84d4ece8730?pvs=21 to learn more about our team and culture!




THE ROLE

You'll expand what Blockit can do and where it shows up in people's lives.

This isn't traditional growth engineering, it's about building new agent capabilities, opening up new surfaces (Slack, mobile, desktop, etc.), and finding ways for Blockit to handle workflows we don't support yet. You'll ship features end-to-end: talking to users, prototyping, building, and measuring. The work spans frontend, backend, and agent engineering.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build new features end-to-end: backend, frontend, and agent integrations

 - Add new tools and capabilities to our agents to handle new workflows

 - Build and improve integrations with external services (Slack, email, calendar providers)

 - Collaborate with design and GTM to figure out what's missing and what's broken for our users

 - Prototype quickly, ship, and iterate based on what you learn

 - Work across the codebase—you'll touch a lot of different systems


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2+ years of industry experience shipping production software

 - Ability to work across the stack as needed

 - Product sense: you care about UX, correctness, and clarity, not just passing tests

 - Pragmatic approach to building: you validate quickly, then harden what works

 - Clear communicator—written and verbal. You'll be working across eng, design, and users


LOCATION

 - San Francisco, CA. On‑site 4 days per week

source_jobs.title: Mid-Market Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BLOCKIT

Blockit is a Sequoia-backed AI time agent.

It learns your preferences and can autonomously coordinate meetings to any degree of complexity (timezones, group scheduling, IRL, etc) similar to an executive assistant – but never goes to sleep and responds instantly. It is the foundation of a massive platform of AI agents that will have access to the world's time. The more people who continue to link their calendars to Blockit, the more powerful our network becomes





ROLE

Blockit is seeking a Mid-Market Account Executive to join our growing team. We're looking for someone with 2+ years of sales experience at a software company, ideally within the horizontal productivity tools space.

You should be hungry, organized and charismatic, and have a track record of high achievement, consistently hitting or exceeding quotas. You are comfortable and excited by the speed of zero-to-one building in a startup environment and bonus points if you have a passion for productivity tools.

Our agent spreads virally which means there is a significant qualified inbound pipeline to farm - we need you to close it.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Reach out to existing inbound users and convert them into customers.

 - Run product demos of our agent, showcasing its capabilities.

 - Delineate between buyer, end-user, and decision-maker in the sales process to effectively navigate deals.

 - Onboard new customers and provide initial support by answering basic FAQs.

 - Share product feedback back with the team to help shape our roadmap.

 - Negotiate and close larger contracts with ACVs ranging from ~$5k - $100k, quarterbacking the entire deal process.

source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Blockit is a Sequoia-backed AI scheduling agent.

We learn your preferences on the fly and autonomously coordinate calendars to any degree of complexity — timezones, group scheduling, IRL, etc. Blockit is like having an executive assistant who never sleeps and responds instantly.

We're a small team serving thousands of users. All customers have come inbound: people encounter Blockit in email or Slack threads, adopt it, and spread it to their teams. We're keeping the team tight — but hiring a few exceptional people as we scale.




ROLE

We're looking for an Engineering Recruiter to own full-cycle hiring for technical roles. You'll partner with engineering leadership to shape roles, source candidates directly, and manage agency relationships. Your job is to make sure we hire exceptional engineers — quickly, and with a great candidate experience.




DAY-TO-DAY

 - Partner with engineering leads to define roles and nail down what great looks like for each hire

 - Source candidates directly — deploying creative strategies to find technical talent that's hard to reach

 - Run a rigorous interview process that sharply assesses skill, drive, and fit

 - Own the candidate lifecycle from first screen to signed offer — including complex negotiations

 - Manage external recruiting partners and hold them to our standards

 - Use data to find bottlenecks and continuously improve our hiring funnel

 - Ensure every candidate — hired or not — walks away with a great impression of Blockit


PROFILE

 - 3+ years of technical recruiting experience, ideally at fast-growing tech companies

 - Track record of sourcing and closing engineering hires

 - Strong grasp of technical skill sets and how engineering orgs work

 - Comfortable managing multiple searches across different disciplines

 - Experience with LinkedIn Recruiter and modern sourcing tools

 - Clear communicator who can manage stakeholders and keep things moving


ABOUT YOU

 - You thrive in fast-paced environments — and actually enjoy the intensity of early-stage startups

 - You're ambitious and treat hard problems as puzzles to solve

 - You're curious — you want to understand how AI works and why scheduling is harder than it looks

 - You're an owner — autonomous, self-directed, and comfortable with ambiguity

 - You're organized, thoughtful, and kind

notes: Sequoia-backed AI time agent with strong engineering focus
